articles.headline: Sydney Taylor scores 10 of her 16 points in the 3rd quarter and the Sky beat the Mercury 77-66
articles.description: — Sydney Taylor scored 10 of her 16 points in the third quarter, Natasha Cloud finished with 11 points and six assists, and the Chicago Sky beat the Phoenix Mercury 77-66 on Tuesday night.

articles.headline: Jessica Shepard secures 3rd triple-double of the season and the Wings beat the Liberty 88-77
articles.description: — Jessica Shepard had 22 points, 12 rebounds and 11 assists for her third triple-double of the season, and the Dallas Wings beat the New York Liberty 88-77 on Tuesday night.

articles.headline: Who has the most triple-doubles in WNBA history? Key stats
articles.description: A triple-double is an impressive feat. Which WNBA players have achieved it? Check out a historical rundown of stat leaders.

images.caption: Alyssa Thomas holds the WNBA record for career triple-doubles.

articles.headline: Sparks' Nneka Ogwumike headlines WNBA All-Star Game reserves
articles.description: Los Angeles Sparks forward Nneka Ogwumike headlines the 12 reserves selected by league coaches Tuesday for the WNBA All-Star Game on July 25 in Chicago.

images.name: Nneka Ogwumike [600x400]
images.caption: Over 440 career WNBA games, Nneka Ogwumike has averaged 16.8 points and 7.5 rebounds and shot 54% from the field.

articles.headline: Fever's Clark hopeful to play Wed., says back-to-back unlikely
articles.description: Fever star Caitlin Clark, who has spent the past two weeks rehabbing from a back injury, said she is "very hopeful" to play Wednesday against the Sparks but admitted it would be difficult for her to play the next day against the Mercury.

images.name: Caitlin Clark [600x400]
images.credit: Photo by Pepper Robinson/NBAE via Getty Images

articles.headline: Guard Chennedy Carter waived by champion Las Vegas Aces
articles.description: Guard Chennedy Carter, the No. 4 pick by the Atlanta Dream in the 2020 draft out of Texas A&M, has been waived by the Aces, the team announced Tuesday. She played in 13 of Las Vegas' 21 games, averaging 12.2 points.

articles.headline: Geno Auriemma says hard fouls on Caitlin Clark not a 'referendum on America'
articles.description: Geno Auriemma says on-court confrontations involving Caitlin Clark should not be viewed as a "referendum on America."

images.caption: Caitlin Clark lands hard, takes hand to the neck

articles.headline: How to watch Robin Roberts, Geno Auriemma WNBA broadcast
articles.description: Robin Roberts and Geno Auriemma turn back the clock by broadcasting Wings vs. Liberty, 30 years after calling ESPN's first WNBA game. Here's how to catch the broadcast.

article.headline: Reese has 21 points, 11 rebounds, and all Dream starters score 16-plus in 108-101 win over Fever
article.description: — Angel Reese had 21 points and 11 rebounds for her 10th double-double of the season, Jordin Canada added 18 points, and the Atlanta Dream beat the Indiana Fever 108-101 on Thursday night.
article.linkText: Reese has 21 points, 11 rebounds, and all Dream starters score 16-plus in 108-101 win over Fever

video.title: Angel Reese knocks down the dagger for the Dream
video.description: Angel Reese knocks down the dagger for the Dream.
video.shortDescription: Angel Reese knocks down the dagger for the Dream.

tracking.trackingName: WNBA_One-Play (Angel Reese knocks down the dagger for the Dream) 2026/06/18 ESHEET

article.story: INDIANAPOLIS -- — Angel Reese had 21 points and 11 rebounds for her 10th double-double of the season, Jordin Canada added 18 points, and the Atlanta Dream beat the Indiana Fever 108-101 on Thursday night. Naz Hillmon and Allisha Gray each scored 17 points and Rhyne Howard added 16 as all five Atlanta starters scored in double figures. Caitlin Clark and Kelsey Mitchell led Indiana (9-6) with 26 points apiece. Aliyah Boston had 23 points and eight rebounds, missing her fifth straight double-double. Mitchell became the eighth player in WNBA history to make 700 career 3-pointers. Atlanta (10-4) led by as many as 13 points in the second half. But Indiana went on a 9-0 run in the fourth quarter to tie it at 93-all on Sophie Cunningham's layup with 5:07 remaining. Mitchell scored the first seven points of the run, including her milestone 3-pointer. Atlanta took advantage of Indiana’s 17th turnover late in the fourth with a fast-break layup to take a 99-95 lead with 2:08 left. Then, Howard scored on Atlanta’s next two possessions to make it 103-97. Reese sealed it by making a layup while being fouled with 23.1 seconds left, completing the three-point play. Reese played the entire second half without picking up a foul after being called for four in the first half. She went to the bench with 5:45 left in the second quarter, but Atlanta responded by outscoring Indiana 19-11 the rest of the half for a 58-49 lead. Clark's signature shoe Clark put her new signature shoes in game action, one day after Nike announced it was releasing the “Caitlin 1” this fall. But Clark, and the racer blue shoes that feature her initials on the tongue, did not log as many minutes as planned after picking up her fifth foul with 8:41 remaining in the fourth. Clark came back into the game at the 3:28 mark, with Indiana trailing 95-93, but she did not attempt another shot to go scoreless in the frame. Clark had been hinting at the shoe’s long-anticipated arrival recently on social media posts. “I think even yesterday, like knowing I was going to post that early in the morning, I didn’t sleep very well because I was just so excited,” Clark said before the game. Clark finished 8 of 17 from the floor, with two made 3-pointers. Up next Atlanta hosts Indiana on Saturday. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/WNBA-basketball
